channel.title: Sort on nonpunctual
channel.description: Recent content in Sort on nonpunctual

item.title: A Haiku On Regular Expressions
item.description:
Info

These posts are being re-created from old Jamf Nation posts not because they are the best but because they may have useful things that were mangled in the Jamf Nation transition to its most recent hosting platform.

That, & they were always supposed to be blog posts anyway… I didn’t have a blog then.


For whatever reason, there was a day when the Mac admins slack was particularly goofy & there was a call for poems. I posted this:

item.title: Dynamic Token Of Static Appreciation
item.description:
Info

NOTE: These posts are being re-created from old Jamf Nation posts not because they are the best but because they may have useful things that were mangled in the Jamf Nation transition to its most recent hosting platform.

That, & they were always supposed to be blog posts anyway… I didn’t have a blog then.


It’s been a while since I posted anything, so, for (future) me & for you I am posting this script that dynamically updates Static Group membership.

item.title: Super Size Me
item.description:
Info

NOTE: These posts are being re-created from old Jamf Nation posts not because they are the best but because they may have useful things that were mangled in the Jamf Nation transition to its most recent hosting platform.

That, & they were always supposed to be blog posts anyway… I didn’t have a blog then.


A while back I posted very excitedly about an update to the du binary. I forgot to post that I actually used it for something…

item.title: Googalogically Speaking
item.description:
Info

NOTE: These posts are being re-created from old Jamf Nation posts not because they are the best but because they may have useful things that were mangled in the Jamf Nation transition to its most recent hosting platform.

That, & they were always supposed to be blog posts anyway… I didn’t have a blog then.


tl;dr: This script is dumb. Do not read this.

Long version: I have seen lots of “curl the package then install the package” scripts. Some are ghastly, some are complicated (ahem, _APS…)
